session.title: Workspaces
session.closeTabConfirm: Close tab {{label}} and all of its panes?
session.noSessionsHint: Create a new session to get started
session.deleteWarning: The tmux session will end. It will remain in the list as "Lost" and can be resumed with the Resume button.
session.duplicateWorkingDir: A session for the same agent is already running in this directory ({{name}})

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.codexOtherMetricsComingSoon: Codex daily activity, model usage, and cost estimates are coming soon
dashboard.codexRateLimitExceeded: Codex rate limit exceeded — wait for reset or purchase credits
dashboard.usageErrorRateLimitedDetail: Another process (e.g. statusline) may be hitting the API
dashboard.usageErrorUnauthorized: Auth error: please log in to Claude Code again
dashboard.statusDanger: Will hit limit in {{time}} at this pace (resets in {{resetTime}} if idle)
dashboard.herdrUpdateCost: Applying it requires restarting the herdr server. All panes are re-created — agent conversations are restored automatically, but running commands are lost.
dashboard.herdrUpdateManualHint: herdr isn't managed by systemd/launchd here, so restart the herdr server manually to apply.
dashboard.grokNoRateLimitInfo: xAI does not expose rate-limit windows locally, so token totals are aggregated from Grok session logs instead of showing cycle utilization.

files.title: File Browser
files.fileTooLarge: File is too large, showing partial content

conversation.title: Conversation
conversation.errorMissingAgent: Session has no agent information. Try refreshing the session list.
conversation.errorUnsupportedAgent: Conversation history is not supported for "{{agent}}".

keyboard.title: Keyboard
keyboard.japaneseInput: Japanese input - Press Enter to send

prompt.searchHint: Enter search words or search with empty to show recent prompts

onboarding.resetConfirm: This will reset the tutorial and reload the page.
onboarding.step1Description: Control Claude Code sessions remotely. Access anytime from your tablet or smartphone.
onboarding.step2Description: Manage multiple Claude Code sessions simultaneously. Tap the menu button to view the session list.
onboarding.step3Description: Monitor usage limits, activity statistics, and cost estimates in real-time.
onboarding.step4Description: Review files modified by Claude Code. View diffs and previews to understand changes.
onboarding.step5Description: View the conversation history with Claude Code. Easily review past exchanges and context.
onboarding.step6Description: Tap the terminal to open the keyboard. Supports Japanese input, 📁 for image upload, and 🔗 for URL listing.
onboarding.step7Description: If the terminal display gets corrupted, tap this button to reset and fix the display.
onboarding.splitPaneDescription: Split the terminal vertically or horizontally to run multiple sessions side by side.
onboarding.sessionItemDescription: Tap to open the session. Long press to change color theme or delete. The status indicator shows if Claude is running.
onboarding.newSessionDescription: Create a new Claude Code session. Choose a working directory to start coding.
onboarding.historyTabDescription: Search and browse past Claude Code sessions. Resume previous conversations or view their history.
onboarding.dashboardTabDescription: Monitor usage limits, activity statistics, and cost estimates in real-time.
onboarding.welcomePrompt: Hello! Welcome to CC Hub. You are Claude Code, being accessed remotely through CC Hub. Please briefly introduce yourself.
onboarding.hookNotConfigured: Hook notifications are missing or incomplete. Add cchub notify to your Claude Code or Codex hook config in your home directory so the CC Hub session list shows accurate status (running / completed / waiting for input) and OS notifications fire when responses finish.
onboarding.hookSetupPrompt:
Please add `cchub notify` to your Claude Code or Codex hook settings in your home directory. Add it to all four events: Stop, PreToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, and PostToolUse (matcher: AskUserQuestion). The hook command is: cchub notify
Claude Code example:
{"hooks":{"Stop":[{"hooks":[{"type":"command","command":"cchub notify"}]}],"PreToolUse":[{"hooks":[{"type":"command","command":"cchub notify"}]}],"UserPromptSubmit":[{"hooks":[{"type":"command","command":"cchub notify"}]}],"PostToolUse":[{"matcher":"AskUserQuestion","hooks":[{"type":"command","command":"cchub notify"}]}]}}
Codex example:
[[hooks.Stop]]
[[hooks.Stop.hooks]]
type = "command"
command = "cchub notify"

[[hooks.PreToolUse]]
[[hooks.PreToolUse.hooks]]
type = "command"
command = "cchub notify"

[[hooks.UserPromptSubmit]]
[[hooks.UserPromptSubmit.hooks]]
type = "command"
command = "cchub notify"

[[hooks.PostToolUse]]
matcher = "AskUserQuestion"
[[hooks.PostToolUse.hooks]]
type = "command"
command = "cchub notify"
Merge this into the existing settings.json or config.toml in your home directory, preserving any existing hooks. Add cchub notify as a new entry or append to the existing hooks array — do not overwrite other settings.
